nav.accountDesc: Sign in to manage datasets, organisations, and protected endpoints.
nav.degradedDesc: One or more services are unavailable — some features may not work.

system.backendUnavailableDesc: Querying, validation, and import actions will fail until the service is reachable.

search.placeholder: Jump to resource IRI or search…

home.title: Triplestore workspace
home.detail: Operate RDF data, queries, imports, and validation from one place.
home.heroTitle: Modern RDF operations without bouncing between tools.
home.heroCopy: Import data, inspect triples, query with SPARQL, validate SHACL reports, and visualize graph neighborhoods from one coherent workspace.
home.searchPlaceholder: Paste a resource IRI or search by subject
home.pulseDesc: Use the workspace as a loop: load data, inspect triples, validate, then drill into the affected graph or resource.
home.triplesDesc: Current store volume available for browse and query operations.
home.graphsDesc: Graph partitions ready for targeted imports, exports, and filtering.
home.datasetsDesc: Curated data groupings with services, ownership, and validation context.
home.workflowActiveDesc: The store has data available for browse and exploration.
home.workflowReadyDesc: Start with import to seed the workspace.
home.flow1: Load or update data through import.
home.flow2: Inspect graph slices in the triple browser.
home.flow3: Use SPARQL for deeper discovery or export.
home.flow4: Run SHACL validation and follow issues back to resources.
home.createFirstDatasetDetail: Establish a managed dataset before loading more graph data.
home.importSourceDataDetail: The store looks empty. Start with an RDF file or update query.
home.inspectGraphDetail: Open connected resources visually to understand structure faster.

workflows.importDesc: Upload RDF files or execute SPARQL updates, then jump straight into review.
workflows.browseDesc: Filter by subject, predicate, object, and graph to understand the store quickly.
workflows.sparqlDesc: Use the editor for ad-hoc discovery, saved query patterns, and exports.
workflows.validateDesc: Run conformance reports and inspect failing nodes in one workflow.

tripleBrowser.title: Triple Browser
tripleBrowser.detail: Inspect triples with filters, paging, and export.
tripleBrowser.subject: Subject
tripleBrowser.iriRequired: Subject, Predicate and Graph filters must be absolute IRIs (e.g. https://example.org/MyResource).
tripleBrowser.noMatchFilters: No triples match the current filters.
tripleBrowser.negOffHint: Exclude rows that match this value (NOT)
tripleBrowser.negOnHint: Excluding matching rows — click to include them again
tripleBrowser.nlNoQuery: No query was generated. Try rephrasing.
tripleBrowser.expandBnodeEmpty: This blank node has nothing more to expand in the current scope.
tripleBrowser.noMappableSub: Resources with WGS84 geometry (and to-scale 3D models) appear here on a map.
tripleBrowser.simpleModeHint: Guided view — plain labels, friendly facets
tripleBrowser.advancedModeHint: Power view — raw IRIs, SPARQL preview, copy-as-query
tripleBrowser.versionSnapshotHint: Choose a version snapshot per dataset in scope
tripleBrowser.globalSearchPlaceholder: Search anything — words, names, values  ·  try AND  OR  NOT  ( )  "phrase"
tripleBrowser.syntaxNote: Operators are UPPERCASE. Matching is case-insensitive across subject, predicate, object & graph.
tripleBrowser.ffHintPart3: button to switch a field between contains, exact and regex.
tripleBrowser.ffHintNeg: Toggle NOT to exclude rows that match instead.
tripleBrowser.llmOfflinePlaceholder: LLM offline — start the gateway to use natural-language search
tripleBrowser.nlAskPlaceholder: Ask in natural language — e.g. “every book published before 2000”
tripleBrowser.equivalentSparql: Equivalent SPARQL for the current filters (approximate)
tripleBrowser.searchSubstringHint: Search looks for the substring across subject, predicate, object and graph IRIs.
tripleBrowser.noGraphDataSub: Import data or relax your filters.

resource.title: Resource detail
resource.detail: Inspect one IRI and follow its relationships.
resource.openInTripleBrowserExact: Open this resource in the Triple Browser (exact match)
resource.viewRawLinkedData: View raw Linked Data (dereference this IRI)
resource.graphHint: its immediate neighbours. Click a node to open that resource.
resource.noAccessibleGraphsDesc: You don't have access to any datasets, so this resource cannot be resolved. Ask an administrator, or load some data via the Datasets page.
resource.iriNotInAnyGraph: This IRI is not in any accessible graph.
resource.iriNotInAnyGraphDesc: No triples found with this resource as subject or object. The IRI may be unknown to the store, or live in a graph you can't access.
resource.outgoingHintAfter: ; each row is a property and its value.
resource.incomingHintAfter: ; each row is another resource and the property it points with.

graphViz.title: Graph explorer
graphViz.detail: Move through connected entities with visual layouts.
graphViz.searchPlaceholder: Search or enter a resource URI…
graphViz.emptyPrompt: Enter a URI or search term to start exploring the graph.
graphViz.emptyHint: Double-click any node to expand its connections.
graphViz.noDataHint: Search for a resource or enter a URI in the sidebar.
graphViz.fitAllTitle: Fit the whole graph in view (F)
graphViz.prettifyTitle: Re-run the layout to tidy the graph (R)
graphViz.pinToggleTitle: Pin or unpin the selected node (P)
graphViz.searchNodesInGraphTitle: Search nodes currently in the graph
graphViz.legendClassTitle: An ontology class (rdfs:Class / owl:Class) — a type that instances belong to.
graphViz.legendConceptTitle: A SKOS concept or concept scheme — a term in a controlled vocabulary.
graphViz.legendResourceTitle: A named individual or instance identified by a URI.
graphViz.legendLiteralTitle: A concrete data value (text, number, date, boolean or geometry). The corner tag shows its kind.
graphViz.legendBNodeTitle: An anonymous node with no global identifier; it groups related values that belong to its parent.
graphViz.typeUriExplain: A globally unique web identifier that names this thing. Open it to discover more about it.
graphViz.typeLiteralExplain: A concrete data value — text, a number, a date, a boolean or a geometry — not a link to another thing.
graphViz.typeBnodeExplain: An anonymous node with no global identifier. It bundles related values (e.g. a geometry, address or distribution) that belong to its parent.
graphViz.inspectorNoProps: No outgoing properties loaded for this node.
graphViz.inspectorExpandHint: Double-click the node in the graph to load more connections.

llmChat.title: Spark
llmChat.detail: Chat about the linked data on this platform — datasets, topics, API services, and the knowledge graphs.
llmChat.example1: How many datasets are there, and what topics do they cover? List them.
llmChat.example2: Is there an API service I can call to answer a question about cities, and how do I call it?
llmChat.example3: What named graphs can I query right now?
llmChat.example4: How many triples are in the Standards demo dataset?
llmChat.example5: Chart the number of triples per named graph as a bar chart.
llmChat.unavailable: The assistant is unavailable. Is an LLM endpoint configured and running?
llmChat.offlineBanner: The LLM endpoint isn't reachable from the server, so answers are unavailable. Configure LLM_GATEWAY_URL (and start your endpoint), then reload.
llmChat.welcomeTitle: What would you like to know?
llmChat.welcomeIntro: I can see the datasets, API services and graphs you have access to. Try one of these:
llmChat.guestNote: You're browsing as a guest, so I can only see public data. Sign in to ask about your own datasets.
llmChat.stopped: Stopped before the answer was finished.
llmChat.composerPlaceholder: Ask about datasets, API services, or the knowledge graphs…
llmChat.disclaimer: Answers are grounded in the data you can access and may run scoped SPARQL queries. The model can make mistakes — verify important results.
llmChat.historyLoginNote: Log in to keep your chat history.
llmChat.aboutGroundingText: Spark only sees the datasets, API services and named graphs you can access, and answers data questions by running read-only SPARQL queries under your permissions.
llmChat.aboutPrivacyText: Chats you save are stored in your account and only visible to you. Administrators can see request metadata (timing, status, a short question preview) — not whole conversations.
llmChat.memoryHint: Standing preferences Spark applies to every chat — e.g. “Answer in Dutch” or “I mostly work with the bridge datasets”.

sparql.title: SPARQL workspace
sparql.detail: Write queries, inspect results, and export findings.
sparql.noMappable: No mappable (WGS84) geometry in these results.
sparql.backToServiceTitle: Return to the API service you opened
sparql.versionSelectTitle: Query the live data or a version snapshot of this dataset
sparql.saveAsApiServiceTitle: Save this query to the dataset's saved queries
sparql.queryingSubset: Querying {selected} of {total} graphs via FROM clauses
sparql.lockedToDataset: This editor is locked to the {name} dataset
sparql.lockedToOrg: This editor is locked to the {name} organisation
sparql.allDatasetsHint: All datasets — querying the whole store
sparql.llmOffline: LLM service offline — start the gateway ({gateway})
sparql.nlPlaceholderOffline: LLM service offline — start the gateway to use natural-language lookup
sparql.nlPlaceholder: Ask in natural language — e.g. “every book published before 2000”
sparql.generatedHint: Generated — run it to confirm, or rate it:
sparql.nlCommentPlaceholder: Optional: add a review comment for this suggestion (helps the training feedback loop)…
sparql.emptyQueryError: The model returned an empty query.
sparql.generationFailed: Generation failed. Is the LLM gateway running?

import.title: Data import
import.detail: Load RDF files or execute SPARQL updates.
import.heading: Data Import
import.signInDesc: You need to be authenticated to import data into the triplestore.
import.dragDrop: Drag & drop RDF files or folders here
import.formatHint: Supported: Turtle, N-Triples, N-Quads, TriG, RDF/XML, JSON-LD
import.sparqlHint: Enter a SPARQL Update query (INSERT DATA, DELETE DATA, LOAD, etc.):
import.noDatasets: No datasets found for this owner.
import.sparqlBypass: SPARQL Updates modify the store directly. No target graph selection needed.
import.replaceArchiveNote: Previous graph is archived by this upload
import.replaceArchiveNotePlural: Previous graphs are archived by this upload
import.versionBumpHint: If the upload is identical to the current data, it's saved as a draft and no new version is published.
import.versionDraftIdentical: Upload identical to current data — saved as draft {version} (no new version published).
import.wherePreviewNote: WHERE-based update — data preview runs a read-only SELECT against the store.
import.noRowsMatched: No rows matched — the WHERE clause returns no results in the current store.
import.opNoPreview: operation — no triple-level preview is shown for graph-management updates. The operation will be sent to the server as-is when you click Import.
import.typeUpdateOrTemplate: Type a SPARQL update or pick a template above.
import.noTriplesPatternHint: clause was detected. If you intended a pattern-based update, make sure your query uses
import.subject: Subject
import.embeddedNotClassified: Embedded graphs could not be classified
import.kindModelTitle: Detected as OWL/RDFS model (classes and properties)
import.noGraphIrisDetected: No graph IRIs detected; data will be uploaded using the embedded graph identifiers.
import.defaultGraphRoutedNote: Triples with no graph label will be imported into this dataset's default graph.
import.graphIriAbsolute: Graph IRI must be an absolute IRI (e.g. https://example.org/my-graph)
import.targetGraphRequired: A target graph IRI is required.
import.preflightForeignTitle: Some graphs are outside this dataset
import.preflightForeignBody: {count} embedded graph(s) would be rejected because they are not under this dataset's namespace. Re-home them to import successfully.
import.preflightMergeWarning: {count} source graphs would be merged into a shared target graph. Rename them to keep them separate.
import.noRoleDetected: No role detected — pick one or leave on auto-detect.
import.autoSplitEnabled: Auto-split enabled — triples will be routed to role-typed sub-graphs on import.
import.looksLikeModel: This looks like a knowledge model (OWL / RDFS / SHACL).
import.vocabularyRegistryHint: Vocabulary files usually belong in the Vocabulary Registry, not a dataset. You can register it there and then link datasets to it.
import.modelRegistryHint: Model files usually belong in the Model Registry, not a dataset. You can register it there and then link datasets to it.
import.datasetNameTaken: A dataset with this name already exists for this owner.
import.noWhereClause: Could not find a WHERE clause to preview.
import.shapesFoundPrompt: {count} shape(s) found in the uploaded graph. Would you like to link this graph as a shapes graph for a dataset?
import.shapesFoundPromptMulti: {count} SHACL shape(s) found in {graphs} imported graph(s). Would you like to link them as a shapes graph for a dataset?
import.noDatasetsWithoutShapes: No datasets without a shapes graph found. You can link manually from the dataset settings.
import.whereUpdateExecuted: WHERE-based update executed successfully. Query results were pattern-matched server-side.

datasets.title: Datasets
datasets.detail: Manage dataset configuration and linked services.
datasets.name: Name
datasets.infoIntro: Datasets contain named graphs that can be linked to data models and vocabularies in the Model & Vocabulary Registry.
datasets.infoVisibility: Visibility — public: anyone can read; members: organisation members only; private: owner only.
datasets.infoSparql: SPARQL sub-endpoint — each dataset exposes its own endpoint at /api/datasets/{id}/sparql.
datasets.infoShacl: SHACL on-write — attach a shapes graph to validate every update before it is committed.
datasets.infoOwnership: Ownership — owned by a personal account or an organisation; org-owned datasets are visible to all org members.
datasets.validateTooltip: Has validation shapes — validate this dataset
datasets.createSubtitle: Create a new named-graph container with its own SPARQL endpoint
datasets.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional — describe what this dataset contains…
datasets.graphRoleHint: Helps categorise how this dataset is used in your knowledge graph
datasets.deleteMessage: This will permanently delete the dataset and all its named graphs from the triplestore. This action cannot be undone.
datasets.bulkDeleteMessage: This will permanently delete {count} {noun} and all their named graphs. This cannot be undone.

datasetDetail.browseTriplesDesc: Filter and inspect raw RDF data in this dataset
datasetDetail.sparqlDesc: Write and run SPARQL queries scoped to this dataset
datasetDetail.noGraphs: No graphs assigned yet. Import data to populate this dataset.
datasetDetail.noServices: No SPARQL services configured yet. Add a service to create a query endpoint.
datasetDetail.versionPickerTitle: View a snapshot version of this dataset
datasetDetail.noDescriptiveMetadataHint2: to add a license, themes, keywords, status and contact details — they enrich the DCAT record and the dataset's linked-data graph.
datasetDetail.apiServicesDesc: Run & manage saved SPARQL API services
datasetDetail.showingSnapshotPost: snapshot — the linked-data graph and sample triples below reflect this version, not the live data.
datasetDetail.privateBadgeTitle: Private — hidden from viewers and the public
datasetDetail.attachShapesGraphTitle: Attach shapes — these travel with the graph and are inherited by any dataset that includes it
datasetDetail.makeGraphVisibleTitle: Make this graph visible to all who can access the dataset
datasetDetail.makeGraphPrivateTitle: Make this graph private — only owner/maintainer/admin can see it
datasetDetail.accessHintIntro: Give people and teams a role on this dataset:
datasetDetail.accessHintAdmin: manages the dataset and its access.
datasetDetail.accessHintOverride: A role set here overrides the one a person inherits from the organisation; access can also come from a team.
datasetDetail.ownerFullAccessTitle: The dataset owner always has full access
datasetDetail.noPeopleAccess: No people have access yet — use “Add person”.
datasetDetail.selectGraphsExposed: Select which graphs this service exposes
datasetDetail.noNamedGraphsYet: No named graphs in this dataset yet.
datasetDetail.attachShapesDatasetTitle: Attach shape graphs at the dataset level (a binding in the validation layer)
datasetDetail.effectiveShapesHint: dataset-level bindings plus shapes inherited from this dataset's graphs
datasetDetail.noShapesAttachedPost: above, or attach shapes to a graph so every dataset that includes it inherits them.
datasetDetail.inheritedFromGraphTitle: Inherited from a graph in this dataset
datasetDetail.runValidationHint: Run SHACL validation to check this dataset against a shapes graph.
datasetDetail.serviceDescPlaceholder: What data does this service expose?
datasetDetail.graphSubsetCaptionHint: — which named graphs this service exposes
datasetDetail.noNamedGraphsAddAfter: No named graphs in this dataset yet. You can add graphs after creating the service.
datasetDetail.shapesGraphBlankHint: Leave blank to validate with the dataset's effective shapes (attached shape graphs, shapes-role graphs, or the configured shapes graph).
datasetDetail.shapesRoleSetToast: Graph role set to Shapes — registered in the SHACL Studio library.
datasetDetail.shapesAutoRegisteredNotice: These shapes are registered in the SHACL Studio library and now apply to this dataset's validation.
datasetDetail.addPersonAria: Add a person to this dataset
datasetDetail.metaReadonlyHint: These fields are automatically maintained and reflected in the DCAT named graph.
datasetDetail.roleHintShapes: SHACL shape graphs used for validation
datasetDetail.fileStorageNotConfigured: File storage is not configured on this server.
datasetDetail.noWriteAccess: You do not have write access to this dataset.
datasetDetail.noDeleteAssetPermission: You do not have permission to delete this asset.
datasetDetail.cannotMakeAssetPublic: Cannot make an asset public in a {visibility} dataset. Make the dataset public first.
datasetDetail.actionNotAllowed: Action not allowed. Try refreshing the page.
datasetDetail.noVisibilityPermission: You do not have permission to change asset visibility.
datasetDetail.mustBeLoggedInVisibility: You must be logged in to change asset visibility.
datasetDetail.viewer3dDesc: Inspect parts on the map and in 3D, with their linked data
datasetDetail.viewerMapDesc: See located features on an interactive map, with their linked data

validation.title: SHACL Validation
validation.detail: Run reports and inspect conformance issues.
validation.noShapesDesc: This dataset has no SHACL shapes configured.
validation.authGateTitle: Sign in to access SHACL Validation
validation.authGateDesc: Validation of named graphs and datasets against SHACL shapes is only available to authenticated users.
validation.noDatasetSelectedDesc: Select a dataset to run validation, edit its SHACL shapes, or review past validation runs.
validation.notValidatedDesc: Run validation to check {name} against its configured SHACL shapes.
validation.noShapesConfiguredDesc: {name} has no SHACL shapes configured. Define shapes first, then validate.
validation.noRunsYetDesc: Validation runs for {name} will appear here.

organisations.title: Organisations
organisations.name: Name
organisations.infoIntro: Organisations group users into teams and provide shared ownership of datasets.
organisations.infoMembership: Membership — members share access to all members-visibility datasets owned by the organisation.
organisations.infoOwnership: Dataset ownership — assign datasets to an organisation so all members can access them.
organisations.infoSlug: URL slug — each organisation has a unique lowercase slug that appears in URLs and cannot be changed.
organisations.infoScopedSparql: Scoped SPARQL — query across all organisation datasets at /api/organisations/{id}/sparql.
organisations.deleteMessage: This will permanently delete the organisation, all datasets it owns, and all associated named graphs from the triplestore. This action cannot be undone.
organisations.createSubtitle: Create a team to share datasets and manage members
organisations.slugTitle: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only
organisations.slugHint: Lowercase, hyphens only. Cannot be changed later.
organisations.descPlaceholder: Briefly describe what this organisation does…
organisations.membersHint: You can invite members after creating the organisation.
organisations.bulkDeleteMessage: This will permanently delete {count} organisation, all datasets they own, and all associated graphs. This cannot be undone.
organisations.bulkDeleteMessagePlural: This will permanently delete {count} organisations, all datasets they own, and all associated graphs. This cannot be undone.

orgDetail.aboutEmptyAfter: to add a homepage, identifier, contact point and a parent organisation — these populate the organisation's linked-data graph.
orgDetail.hierNoSubUnitsManage: Create another organisation and set this one as its parent, or edit an existing org's metadata.
orgDetail.noDatasetsBefore: No datasets yet. Create one above or import data via
orgDetail.accessMatrixTitle: Manage per-dataset access for members and groups
orgDetail.matrixCaptionIntro: Assign each member or group a role per dataset:
orgDetail.matrixCaptionAdmin: manages the dataset and its access.
orgDetail.matrixCaptionDefaultAfter: to inherit the principal's organisation role.
orgDetail.matrixNoDatasets: This organisation has no datasets yet, so there's no access to assign. Create a dataset above first.
orgDetail.inviteRoleViewer: Viewer — read-only access to public datasets
orgDetail.inviteHintOwner: Owners can add/remove members and edit organisation settings.
orgDetail.inviteHintMember: Members can access organisation datasets marked "members" visibility.
orgDetail.inviteHintViewer: Viewers can only access datasets with public visibility.
orgDetail.apiServicesTitle: Saved SPARQL queries exposed as APIs for this team
orgDetail.deleteGroupMessage: This will remove the group and all its members.
orgDetail.noTeamMembers: No members in this team yet.
orgDetail.addPersonToTeam: Add a person to this team
orgDetail.teamHintAdmin: Admins can manage this team's members.
orgDetail.teamHintMember: Members get edit access to datasets shared with this team.
orgDetail.teamHintViewer: Viewers get read-only access to datasets shared with this team.
orgDetail.everyoneInTeam: Everyone is already in this team.

login.title: Sign in
login.heading: Login
login.subtitle: Sign in to Open Triplestore
login.mfaSubtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to finish signing in.
login.mfaRecoveryHint: Lost your device? Enter one of your recovery codes instead.
login.passkeyFailed: Passkey sign-in failed — try again or use your password.

register.title: Register
register.detail: Create an account for management workflows.
register.heading: Register
register.subtitle: Create your Open Triplestore account
register.usernameStartError: Username must start with a letter or digit.
register.usernameCharsetError: Only letters, digits, '.', '_' and '-' are allowed.
register.emailDomainError: The email domain looks invalid (e.g. use name@example.org).
register.registrationDisabled: Self-registration is disabled on this server. Ask an administrator for an account.
register.verifyBody: We sent a confirmation link to {email}. Open it to verify your email address.
register.verifyNoSmtpHint: This server has no mail relay configured - the confirmation link is written to the server log.

settings.title: Settings
settings.detail: Manage your profile and API tokens.
settings.publicProfileHint: Other users can find and add you to datasets and organisations.
settings.privateProfileHint: Your profile is hidden. Only admins and members of shared resources can see you.
settings.dangerZoneHint: These actions affect your account permanently. They cannot be undone.
settings.pauseAccountHint: Deactivate your account. Your data is preserved but you won't be able to log in. An admin can reactivate your account.
settings.deleteAccountHint: Permanently remove your account and all associated data. This cannot be undone.
settings.superAdminCannotSelfDelete: Super admin accounts cannot be self-deleted. Contact another super admin to manage this account.
settings.apiTokensHint: API tokens allow programmatic access. Store them securely — they are shown only once.
settings.scopeReadDesc: SPARQL queries (SELECT, ASK, CONSTRUCT, DESCRIBE), download graphs, browse datasets / organisations / models / vocabularies, full-text search, GeoSPARQL, SHACL reports, DCAT catalog.
settings.scopeReadNote: Cannot write or modify any data.
settings.scopeWriteDescPost: , plus SPARQL Update, upload RDF files, create/delete datasets and graphs, manage organisations, upload and publish models and vocabularies.
settings.scopeAdminDescPost: , plus user management, role assignment, and token revocation.
settings.newTokenCreated: New token created! Copy it now — it won't be shown again.
settings.name: Name
settings.revokeTokenMessage: The token will stop working immediately and cannot be recovered.
settings.deactivateModalBody1: Your account will be deactivated. You will be logged out immediately. Your datasets will be made private and you will be removed from all organisations.
settings.deactivateModalBody2: An admin can reactivate your account if needed.
settings.purgeModalBody: Your account, datasets, and all personal data will be permanently deleted. You will be removed from all organisations.
settings.changeEmailBody: A confirmation link will be sent to the new address; the change applies once confirmed.
settings.emailChangePending: Confirmation link sent to {email}. The change applies once confirmed.
settings.emailPendingHint: Pending change to {email} - waiting for confirmation.
settings.verificationSent: Confirmation email sent - check your inbox.
settings.twoFactorHint: Require a 6-digit code from an authenticator app when signing in with your password.
settings.twoFactorOnHint: Signing in requires your password plus a code from your authenticator app.
settings.twoFactorOffHint: Your account is protected by your password only.
settings.twoFactorSetupBody: Scan the QR code with an authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator, 1Password, Aegis), then enter the 6-digit code it shows.
settings.recoveryCodesOnce: These codes are shown only once.
settings.recoveryCodesBody: Store them somewhere safe - each one can be used a single time to sign in if you lose your authenticator.
settings.twoFactorDisableBody: Confirm with your password and a current code (or a recovery code). Your account falls back to password-only sign-in.
settings.passkeysHint: Sign in without a password using your device's screen lock, fingerprint, or a hardware security key.
settings.passkeyAddBody: Give the passkey a recognisable name, then follow your browser's prompts to create it.
settings.passkeyRemoveBody: This passkey will no longer be able to sign in to your account. Confirm with your password.
settings.passkeyUnsupported: This browser does not support passkeys.

admin.title: User Management
admin.detail: Manage user accounts, roles, and access.
admin.deactivateConfirmMessage: Their tokens will be revoked. An admin can reactivate the account later.
admin.purgeConfirmMessage: This removes the account entirely and cannot be undone.
admin.grantPublishPermission: Grant publish permission (can upload & publish model and vocabulary versions)
admin.publishPermission: Publish permission (can upload & publish model and vocabulary versions)
admin.publishPermissionHint: Admins and super-admins always have this permission regardless of this setting.
admin.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters

graphList.title: Named graphs
graphList.detail: Review graph inventory and graph-level actions.
graphList.noGraphs: No named graphs found in the store.
graphList.deleteWarning: This will permanently delete all triples in:
graphList.versionSelectTitle: List the live graphs or a version snapshot's graphs
graphList.infoIntro: are IRIs that identify distinct sets of triples within the triplestore. They form the storage foundation for datasets and provide a way to organize, query, and manage RDF data by logical context.
graphList.infoIriIdentifierDesc: each named graph has a unique IRI. The default graph is unnamed.
graphList.infoTripleStorageDesc: named graphs contain the actual RDF triples (subject, predicate, object).
graphList.infoSparqlQueriesPre: query a specific graph with the
graphList.infoSparqlQueriesPost: clause in SPARQL, or use SPARQL sub-endpoints scoped to datasets.
graphList.infoOwnershipDesc: graphs can belong to datasets (which have visibility and validation rules) or exist independently.
graphList.versionBannerPost: snapshot's named graphs. Triple counts may be unavailable for snapshot graphs.
graphList.detectRolesTitle: Probe graph contents to suggest a role
graphList.roleDetectedTitle: Detected from graph contents — not yet saved
graphList.roleInferredTitle: Guessed from the graph IRI — not yet saved
graphList.saveRoleTitle: Save this role on the dataset
graphList.partialTitle: {count} graph(s) have no available triple count
graphList.newDatasetFromSelectionTitle: Create a new dataset containing the selected graphs
graphList.bulkDeleteMessage: This will permanently delete {count} named graph(s) and all their triples from the triplestore. This cannot be undone.
graphList.newDatasetHint: A new dataset will be created and the selected graphs will be linked to it.
graphList.newNamedGraphSubtitle: Register a new named graph IRI in the triplestore
graphList.ngHintPre: A unique IRI that identifies this named graph. Use a URL-style IRI such as

shaclStudio.title: SHACL Studio
shaclStudio.detail: Author shape graphs, run validation pipelines, and review results.
shaclStudio.lede: One place to author SHACL shape graphs, run validation pipelines, and publish dataset+shapes for external form platforms.
shaclStudio.step1Body1: in the Library — visually, in Turtle, or drafted from your data. Browse the
shaclStudio.step1Body2: tab to filter by node or property shapes.
shaclStudio.step2Title: Explore visually in a graph viewer
shaclStudio.step2Body1: — open any shape graph and click
shaclStudio.step2Body2: to inspect its node & property shapes on the canvas.
shaclStudio.step3Body: that validates one or more datasets against your shape graphs, on demand, on every write, or on a schedule.
shaclStudio.step4Body2: toggle — non-conforming writes will be rejected with a 422 + report.
shaclStudio.step5Body2: endpoint an external form platform can load with its attached shapes.

shaclShapes.title: Shapes editor
shaclShapes.detail: Manage SHACL shapes for validation workflows.
shaclShapes.heading: SHACL Shapes
shaclShapes.writeShapes: Write shapes in the editor and save.
shaclShapes.editorHint: Turtle syntax · Ctrl+Enter or Save button to apply

adminSecurity.heading: Security & Access Control
adminSecurity.providersHint: Configure Azure AD, OIDC, or SAML providers. SSO buttons appear on the login page once a provider is enabled.
adminSecurity.autoProvisionDesc: Create a local account automatically the first time someone signs in through this provider. When off, only users who already have an account can sign in.
adminSecurity.enabledDesc: Show this provider's sign-in button on the login page. Turn off to hide it without deleting the configuration.
adminSecurity.providersEmpty2: Add one above to enable Azure AD, OIDC, or SAML login buttons.
adminSecurity.endpointEmpty2: All authenticated users can reach any endpoint they have a valid token for.
adminSecurity.graphHint: Grant per-principal read / write / admin access to named graphs. Grants are additive.
adminSecurity.graphEmpty2: Named graphs follow dataset-level visibility by default.
adminSecurity.tripleHint: Label individual triples with a virtual named graph IRI. Access to that IRI is then governed by Graph ACL rules above.
adminSecurity.labelGraphIriHint: (virtual named graph; control access via Graph ACL)
adminSecurity.tripleEmpty2: Labels allow per-triple visibility control beyond graph-level ACL.
adminSecurity.deleteProviderMessage: Users signed in via it will keep their local accounts.
adminSecurity.revokeGraphMessage: The principal will lose access to the graph immediately.

apiDocs.title: API documentation
apiDocs.couldNotLoad: Could not load the API description.
apiDocs.loadFailed: Failed to load the API description.

apiServices.title: API Services
apiServices.leadIntro: Saved SPARQL queries exposed as parameterised HTTP APIs for this {scope}.
apiServices.leadGrouped: Services from this {scope}'s public datasets appear here too, grouped by where they read.
apiServices.signinBrowse: You can browse and run these API services.
apiServices.signinRights: with editor or owner rights to create, edit, version, or delete them.
apiServices.noServicesCreate: Create one, or save the current query from the SPARQL editor.
apiServices.acrossAllDatasetsGroup: Across all datasets in this group
apiServices.servedNote: This query returns tabular bindings (SELECT/ASK), so an RDF serialisation doesn’t apply — showing the SPARQL results instead.
apiServices.noTests: No tests yet — runs automatically when the dataset gains a new version.
apiServices.openInEditorTitle: Edit and run ad-hoc in the SPARQL editor
apiServices.fixWithLlmTitle: The service errored or returned nothing — ask the LLM for a fix
apiServices.llmOffline: LLM service offline — start the gateway
apiServices.nlPlaceholder: Describe the query in natural language — e.g. “every book published before 2000”
apiServices.nlPlaceholderOffline: LLM service offline — start the gateway to use AI generation
apiServices.nlEmptyQuery: The model returned an empty query.
apiServices.nlGenerationFailed: Generation failed. Is the LLM gateway running?
apiServices.testRunTitle: Run this query now against your accessible data
apiServices.testRunHint: Previews live against your accessible graphs; placeholders use the test parameters below.
apiServices.paramsDetectedNotDeclared: Detected in the query but not declared:
apiServices.paramsDeclaredNotUsed: Declared but not used in the query:
apiServices.testParamsLabel: Test parameters (JSON, used for the test run and automatic version testing)
apiServices.formatsHintPre: Callers choose one per request via the
apiServices.formatsHintPost: header. SELECT/ASK queries answer in tabular formats; CONSTRUCT/DESCRIBE in RDF.
apiServices.vbHintEdit: Saving creates a new immutable revision. The name + notes are recorded as linked data and linked to the dataset, like a commit.
apiServices.vbHintNew: The name + notes are recorded as linked data alongside the service, like the first commit.
apiServices.repairSaved: Repaired query saved as a new revision
apiServices.restoreConfirm: Restore revision {revision} as a new revision? The current query is kept in history.
apiServices.restoreSuccess: Restored revision {revision} as a new revision
apiServices.deleteConfirm: Delete API service "{name}"? Its revisions and test history will be removed.
apiServices.testParamsInvalid: Test parameters must be valid JSON, e.g. {example}
apiServices.fillTestParams: Fill in “Test parameters” (JSON) for every '{{'placeholder'}}' before test-running.

docEditor.heading: Documentation editor
docEditor.hintBefore: Create and edit in-app documentation. Docs marked
docEditor.hintAfter: are hidden from regular users (the API returns 404 for them). Built-in docs you edit here are preserved across upgrades.
docEditor.deleteConfirm: Delete doc "{slug}"? This cannot be undone.

documentation.emptyBody: Documentation pages will appear here once they are published.

documentation.detail: Supported standards, capabilities, and usage guide for the Open Triplestore platform.

modelRegistry.heading: Model & Vocabulary Registry
modelRegistry.infoIntro: The Model Registry provides version-controlled storage for RDF/OWL/SHACL data models, following a draft → staged → published lifecycle.
modelRegistry.infoUpload: Upload any supported RDF format (Turtle, RDF/XML, JSON-LD, OWL/XML…)
modelRegistry.infoDraft: Draft versions can be compared and reviewed before staging and publishing
modelRegistry.infoDiff: Diff viewer highlights added (green) and removed (red) triples between versions
modelRegistry.infoPublished: Published versions are immutable; the latest is served at /api/data-models/{id}/latest/data
modelRegistry.newDataModelSubtitle: Register a version-controlled OWL/SHACL data model
modelRegistry.titleHint: A human-readable name for this data model
modelRegistry.namespaceHint: The base IRI for classes and properties defined in this model
modelRegistry.descriptionPlaceholder: Briefly describe the domain and purpose of this data model…
modelRegistry.publicDesc: Anyone can read version data without authentication
modelRegistry.deleteConfirmMessage: This will remove all version metadata. Named graph data is preserved unless you delete it manually.
modelRegistry.bulkDeleteMessage: This will remove all version metadata for {count} model. Named graph data is preserved unless deleted separately.
modelRegistry.bulkDeleteMessagePlural: This will remove all version metadata for {count} models. Named graph data is preserved unless deleted separately.
modelRegistry.title: Model Registry
modelRegistry.detail: Browse and manage versioned data models

pipelineEditor.descriptionPlaceholder: Description — what does this pipeline check?
pipelineEditor.whatToValidateHint: Pick any mix of datasets, named graphs, and shape graphs. Datasets and graphs are validated against their shapes; a shape graph here is meta-validated against SHACL-SHACL.
pipelineEditor.noShapeGraphs: No shape graphs in the Library yet.
pipelineEditor.validateWithHint: The shape graphs used as validators; their results merge into one report. Optional when your targets already carry shapes — datasets and graphs inherit shapes bound to them in the validation layer.
pipelineEditor.noShapeGraphsLibrary: No shape graphs in the Library yet.
pipelineEditor.runInference: Run SHACL-AF inference before validating (materialises rule-derived triples; manual/scheduled runs only)
pipelineEditor.maxResults: Max results per run (blank = unlimited; advisory)
pipelineEditor.visibilityMembers: Members — any signed-in user can read
pipelineEditor.runOnWrite: Run on every write to any in-scope graph
pipelineEditor.cronExpression: Cron expression (5 fields: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week, UTC)
pipelineEditor.gatingActiveDescBefore: Writes to in-scope graphs that violate this pipeline's threshold ({threshold}) will be
pipelineEditor.gatingActiveDescAfter: . Make sure your data already conforms before flipping this on.
pipelineEditor.summaryShapeGraphCount: {count, plural, one {# shape graph} other {# shape graphs}}
pipelineEditor.scheduleHourlyDesc: Every hour, at the top of the hour
pipelineEditor.errorNoTargets: Pick at least one thing to validate — a dataset, graph, or shape graph.
pipelineEditor.errorNoShapeGraphs: Pick at least one shape graph, or a target that carries its own shapes.
pipelineEditor.toastViolationsFound: {count, plural, one {# violation found} other {# violations found}}
pipelineEditor.confirmDelete: Delete pipeline "{name}"? Run history will also be deleted.
pipelineEditor.confirmGateWrites: Enabling "Gate writes" means writes that violate this pipeline will be REJECTED (HTTP 422 + report). Are you sure?
pipelineEditor.nothingSelected: Nothing selected yet — pick datasets, graphs or shape graphs below.
pipelineEditor.pipelineValidatorCount: {count, plural, one {+# pipeline validator} other {+# pipeline validators}}
pipelineEditor.validatedByOnlyPipeline: {count, plural, one {validated by # pipeline validator} other {validated by # pipeline validators}}
pipelineEditor.noValidators: no shapes — nothing will be validated
pipelineEditor.noValidatorsHint: Pick a shape graph under "Validate with", or bind shapes to this target in the Shapes Library.
pipelineEditor.summaryNoValidators: none — relies on shapes bound to targets

pipelinesList.heading: Validation pipelines
pipelinesList.intro: Saved data scope + composed shape graphs + triggers. Runnable on demand, on every write, or on a schedule — and able to reject non-conforming writes.
pipelinesList.emptyDesc: Once you have shape graphs in the Library, compose them into a pipeline that runs on demand, on every write, or on a schedule.
pipelinesList.failedIssues: Failed — {count, plural, one {# issue} other {# issues}}
pipelinesList.violationsFound: {count, plural, one {# violation found} other {# violations found}}

shaclResults.noRunsYetDesc: Run a pipeline or validate a dataset to see results here.

shaclResults.noIssuesForShapes: No issues for the configured shapes.
shaclResults.noReportPayload: No report payload available for this run.

datasetMetadataDialog.nameHelp: The human-readable title of the dataset.
datasetMetadataDialog.descriptionHelp: A short summary of what the dataset contains and who it's for.
datasetMetadataDialog.visibilityHelp: Who can see this dataset. Private = only you / your organisation; Members = signed-in members; Public = anyone.
datasetMetadataDialog.iconHelp: A square-ish image shown next to the dataset name and in listings.
datasetMetadataDialog.bannerHelp: A wide header image shown across the top of the dataset page.
datasetMetadataDialog.licenseHelp: The legal terms under which others may reuse this dataset. Pick a known license or paste a custom IRI.
datasetMetadataDialog.licensePlaceholder: Type to search, or paste a license IRI
datasetMetadataDialog.keywordsHelp: Free-text tags consumers can search by. Type and press Enter or comma to add.
datasetMetadataDialog.themesHelp: Top-level subject categories from the EU data-theme vocabulary. Pick all that apply, or add a custom IRI.
datasetMetadataDialog.admsHelp: Lifecycle status of the dataset, from the W3C ADMS vocabulary.
datasetMetadataDialog.versionNotesHelp: A short note about this version — what changed since the previous release.
datasetMetadataDialog.contactNameHelp: Person or team responsible for this dataset.
datasetMetadataDialog.contactEmailHelp: Where consumers can reach the data steward.
datasetMetadataDialog.contactUrlHelp: A web page where users can ask questions or report issues.
datasetMetadataDialog.spatialHelp: Geographic region the data is about, ideally as a GeoNames IRI.
datasetMetadataDialog.landingPageHelp: Human-readable page about the dataset (e.g. on your data portal).
